title: Absit

{{Quote|Shortly after the Hammers were instrumental in finally suppressing the Yatir, the indigenous species of the planet Absit, Johans was promoted to high colonel and the Hammers were recalled to Brintooin to train and build up to regimental strength.|Special unit threat assessment report by New Republic Captain Zgorth'sth|<ref name="Journal15">{{JournalCite|15|Special Military Unit Intelligence Update}}</ref>}}
'''Absit''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE" /> located in the [[Absit system/Legends|Absit system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Tunka sector/Legends|Tunka sector]].<ref name="EAOC Absit" /> It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Yatir]].<ref name="IS2" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Yatir were sympathetic to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="IS2" /> At some point before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]<ref name="Journal15" /> of [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] finally<ref name="Journal15" /> [[Subjugation of the Yatir|subjugated]] Absit<ref name="CSWE" /> and the Yatir, with the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]]<ref name="IS2" /> [[Vanguard armor regiment/Legends|vanguard armor regiment]] known as the [[Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit/Legends|Imperial Hammers Elite Armor Unit]] being instrumental in the operation.<ref name="Journal15" /> The Empire subsequently used the native population of Absit for [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] labor.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zgorth'sth]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] noted the Imperial subjugation of Absit in a threat assessment report focusing on various special units.<ref name="Journal15" />

==Behind the scenes==
Absit was first mentioned in the [[1989]] ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'', written by [[Greg Gorden]] and published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Imperial Sourcebook">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Absit [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square I-19, although the system was also erroneously placed in grid square J-13 as part of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] in the Appendix section of the book.<ref name="TEA" />

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed the Absit system—as well as the [[Dalchon system/Legends|Dalchon]], [[Mediraan system|Mediraan]], "Ryluth" (a misspelling of the [[Ryloth system/Legends|Ryloth system]]), [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo]], and [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin systems]]—in a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] named the "Outer Rim Territory." Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to ''The Essential Atlas'' has since placed all of those [[Star/Legends|star]] systems save Mediraan in sectors other than the Absit system's Tunka sector.<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref>
